behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: This is a pure deterministic function: given the same parameters, it must always return the same key, and different parameters must never map to the same key. Failures (non-determinism or collisions) are fully deterministic and detectable by direct unit testing with various parameter combinations (empty, single, multiple, special characters, unicode, reordered). No I/O, mocking, or external systems are involved. The function boundary contains the entire behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test with parametrized cases: call generate_cache_key() with identical parameter sets multiple times and verify output is always identical; call with diverse distinct parameter sets and verify no two inputs produce the same output. Test edge cases: empty params, special characters, unicode, parameter order variance, and large parameter sets.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: This is a lookup-based calculation function that maps data category to TTL value. Failures (wrong TTL, wrong rule applied) are deterministic: given category X, the output must always be the configured TTL for X. Mocking is unnecessary since there are no dependencies—just configuration and logic. No I/O or external systems. Testing all categories with their expected TTLs covers the entire behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test with parametrized cases: for each configured data category, call compute_ttl(category) and verify the returned TTL matches the configured freshness rule. Include boundary cases (minimum/maximum TTL, categories with same TTL, categories with 0 or very large TTL) and verify the mapping is exact.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: While the switch logic could be unit-tested with a mocked memory measurement, doing so would miss failures in the actual memory-measurement mechanism itself. MOCK-HIDE: mocking memory always returns controlled values, hiding bugs in real memory tracking. REAL-INFRA: the behavior requires actual cache growth under realistic conditions to trigger the measurement and verify the threshold comparison works. Testing must fill the actual cache and observe the strategy change—this is integration of the measurement system with the eviction selection logic.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test: incrementally populate the cache with entries while monitoring its size or memory usage. Once cache usage exceeds the threshold specified in configuration, add additional entries and verify that get_eviction_strategy() returns LFU instead of LRU. Verify the strategy reverts to LRU once usage falls below threshold. Must use the actual cache implementation with real in-process memory tracking or realistic cache-size tracking.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: mocking the database write would not test whether the application's actual detection mechanism (triggers, CDC, or polling) works. MOCK-HIDE: mocking allows arbitrary control over 'when invalidation happens', hiding failures in the real notification pathway. REAL-INFRA: correctness depends on the actual PostgreSQL behavior and the row-to-cache-key derivation working on real data. The behavior spans multiple systems: database, cache, and application logic interacting. Testing requires a real (or realistic test) PostgreSQL instance, real cache entries, and verification that the invalidation mechanism actually fires in response to writes.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test: insert or update a row in the PostgreSQL table (either directly or via application API). Verify that all cache entries whose keys correspond to the affected row(s) are removed from the cache, while non-matching entries remain. Test single-row writes, bulk writes, and partial row updates. Requires real PostgreSQL and real cache instances with actual data to verify both the key-matching logic and the notification mechanism.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: This tests the correctness of a deterministic algorithm on the cache's internal data structure. Failures (wrong entry chosen, wrong algorithm applied) are fully deterministic: given a cache at capacity with a known access pattern, LRU must evict the least-recently-touched entry and LFU must evict the least-frequently-accessed one. No external systems, no I/O, no concurrency dependencies. The behavior is entirely contained within the cache object and its eviction logic.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test with separate test cases for LRU and LFU: create a cache instance, populate it to full capacity with entries having distinct access timestamps and frequencies. For LRU, set up recency patterns (some entries touched recently, others not), then add a new entry and verify the least-recently-used entry is evicted. For LFU, set up frequency patterns (some entries accessed often, others rarely), then add a new entry and verify the least-frequently-used entry is evicted. Repeat with varied access patterns to ensure the algorithm is correct under different scenarios.
